## What happens

<!-- What you saw. Keep this to observation. -->

## Where

<!-- Point in the game, party, and how you got there. A savestate or .srm
     helps a lot. If you have a test/probe that reproduces it, even better. -->

## What you expected

## Mechanism (optional — and please mark your confidence)

<!-- If you have a theory about the cause, it is very welcome. Please say
     whether you VERIFIED it (cite the file:line in the disassembly, or a
     probe run) or whether you are INFERRING it from the symptom.

     Both are useful. Mixing them up is what CONTRIBUTING.md's "read the
     source, don't infer a mechanism" rule exists to prevent — a
     confidently-worded guess tends to get quoted later as established
     fact. "I don't know why" is a perfectly good answer. -->

## Version

<!-- Release tag or commit SHA. `git rev-parse --short HEAD` -->
